title: Build a dashboard using the Infinity data source

blocks.content: A Grafana dashboard is a set of one or more panels, organized and arranged into one or more rows, that provide an at-a-glance view of related information. These panels are created using components that query and transform raw data from a data source into visualizations.

A data source can be an SQL database, Grafana Loki, Grafana Mimir, or an API endpoint. It can even be a basic CSV file. Data source plugins take a query you want answered, retrieve the data from the data source, and reconcile the differences between the data model of the data source and the data model of Grafana dashboards.

The instructions in this milestone are based on the publicly available [Traffic density](https://thingspeak.com/channels/38629/feed.csv) CSV file, which has the following structure.

```csv
created_at,entry_id,field1,field2
2025-04-07 12:19:26 UTC,18706390,6.000000,20.000000
2025-04-07 12:19:41 UTC,18706391,4.000000,19.000000
2025-04-07 12:19:56 UTC,18706392,8.000000,25.000000
```
To build a dashboard with the Infinity data source, complete the following steps:
From the navigation menu, click **Dashboards**.
Click **New** and select **New dashboard**.
Add a new panel and open the panel editor.
In the query section at the bottom of the screen, click the **Data source** dropdown and search for your Infinity data source.
Select your Infinity data source from the list.
In the **Type** dropdown, select **CSV**.
Set **Method** to **GET**.

In the **URL** field, enter the URL of your CSV data.

For example: `https://thingspeak.com/channels/38629/feed.csv`
Expand **Parsing options & Result fields**.
Under **Advanced Options**, set the **Delimiter** field to `,`.

Click **Add Columns** and add a column for each field:

- **Column Name** `created_at` / **Title** `Created at` / **Format as** `Time`
- **Column Name** `field1` / **Title** `Density of Westbound Cars` / **Format as** `Number`
- **Column Name** `field2` / **Title** `Density of Eastbound Cars` / **Format as** `Number`
In the right pane, click the visualization picker at the top and select **Table**.
Click **Back to dashboard** in the top-left of the panel editor.
Click **Save dashboard** in the toolbar, enter a name, and click **Save**.
The dashboard is now available for others to see.

---

### More to explore (optional)

At this point in your journey, you can explore the following paths:

- [Modify dashboard settings](https://grafana.com/docs/grafana-cloud/visualizations/dashboards/build-dashboards/modify-dashboard-settings/)
- [Manage dashboards](https://grafana.com/docs/grafana-cloud/visualizations/dashboards/manage-dashboards/)
- [Filtering data / Using template variables in Query](https://grafana.com/docs/plugins/yesoreyeram-infinity-datasource/latest/query/filters/)
